title: The Accountant
summary: A decent action film with a curious angle, it does fairly well with a delicate subject, and while it certainly runs wild with its plot, it pays good service to illustrating and elevating something many people live with—that being autism. Lots of punch and surprising shifts and twists.

title: The Adjustment Bureau
summary: A delight through and through, Matt Damon and Emily Blunt are stunning together in this with chemistry that has a real deep hook. Some bits of the plot speed along in ways that feel a bit rushed, but overall it’s a thrilling concept that gives you a compelling reason to root for the protagonists’ success.

title: Ad Astra
summary: Ad Astra is a film that has some really intriguing and engaging concepts behind it, but fails to live up to the hype promised by such high aims. There are endless hoops to jump through, and so few of them feel worth the investment.

title: The Adventures of Tintin
summary: It was just weird; that’s the first thing I’d say. The animation style, while certainly distinct, was a miss for me, and left me thinking back to The Polar Express and how much I detested the film adaptation. Watching this isn’t like getting your fingernails pulled out with pliers, but it’s not a great use of your time, either.

title: A Few Good Men
summary: As a not so good man once said, “You can’t handle the truth.” Well, in this case maybe you can: the truth is that this is a pretty damn great film. A legal drama of heavy movement and loud rebuttals, the cast does a great job in selling their characters, and their animosities.

title: After
summary: Sappy, predictable, and droll - it is just about exactly what you’d expect from a romantic drama torn from the depths of internet fan fiction. There’s a distinct lack of chemistry and the writing is thin enough to tear when blown on.

title: After We Collided
summary: See the review for 2019’s After for a strikingly similar review. These movies seem to all be cut from the same cloth, and they’re definitely not worth the money to see either in theaters, or at home. Was held at gunpoint to watch them, and it was probably a better idea to take the bullet.

title: Air Force One
summary: A classic thriller full of action, suspense, and charged emotions as Harrison Ford’s President James Marshall takes the fight to the enemies who hijack Air Force One, you can’t go wrong with this if you want an engaging and fast paced movie night.

title: Airplane!
summary: An all time classic hit comedy film that will still have a place even fifty years from now. This sort of dry humor speaks to the heart of many people’s unspoken feelings about life and travel in the modern age, and it’s great to be able to laugh about it.

title: A Knight’s Tale
summary: A genuinely heart warming, thrilling, and stunningly delightful film through and through; this is one you can watch on repeat no matter the season or mood. It’s got a fantastic soundtrack, perfect casting, and tremendous chemistry between the actors and the characters they portray. A stellar choice for any movie night.

title: Alexander
summary: Whatever you can imagine as the worst a Hollywood film could be, double that and you’re likely to come close to the 2004 Alexander, starring some fairly well known names that—even with their renown and prestige—can’t save this film from itself. The extended, three hour final cut is, perhaps, the only version worth watching, and even then it’s not worth watching at all. Skip.

title: Alien
summary: On rewatching for perhaps the sixth time, there’s still as much a palpable sense of discomfort and suspense as the first watch through. This is one of those horror movies that has both the slow, grinding build up, and the well deserved pay off at the end. It’s a strong start to the franchise because it keeps things simple: the heroes simply need to survive.

title: Aliens
summary: A thrill that’s just as exciting as its predecessor, Aliens goes the next step of evolving the franchise, of telling new stories within a familiar world. The stakes are just as high, but this time around the audience, and the main character, are fully in the know. There’s less built up to what exactly we’re dealing with, and more a suspenseful ride to the conclusion of how the hell will it get resolved.

title: Alien3
summary: This is one of those ‘not so great’ sequels. It’s loud and suspenseful, but doesn’t do anything new and cool enough to keep it interesting.

title: Alien: Resurrection
summary: Not worth watching. Skip it; please, for the love of god just skip it.

title: Alien Vs. Predator
summary: You don’t sit down for AVP expecting a classical drama or grand, nuanced and moving story. When you sit down for this movie you do so because you’re ready to watch gruesome deaths, exciting fights, and of course the all too terrifying sights of watching an alien parasite stick its ovipositor down someone’s throat to lay eggs in their chest. For all of those things, AVP delivers.

title: Prometheus
summary: A new take on an old concept isn’t a surefire path to success. Sometimes, however, a director can pull it off. Prometheus isn’t one of those success stories. As a stand alone feature entirely disconnected from the Alien series, it could likely make a decent mark, but it draws on too many of the same tricks and twists as the source material, and it doesn’t do enough to distinguish itself as something new. It’s just sort of tiring.

title: Alien: Covenant
summary: In a stark contrast to the film preceding it, Covenant delivers an aggressively paced venture into new depths of horror and thrill as it blazes a new path through uncharted territory. Where Prometheus hemmed a bit too close to the originals without distinguishing itself, Covenant effectively sells a new take on familiar material, adding new twists and terrors that are sure to keep audiences engaged.

title: Alien: Romulus
summary: A bang up thriller with everything one should want from an Alien film. It leans heavily on elements from the originals, while also paying due respect to the prequels that have not performed all that well. There are some genuinely terrifying elements to this film, and every moment of it is slick and tremendous.

title: All the Old Knives
summary: Not a terrible film, in truth, but one that stumbles and staggers despite having a genuinely intriguing concept to build off on. There’s decent chemistry between cast members, few as they may be, but there are things that just don’t execute quite well, leaving one a bit less than completely satisfied.

title: All Quiet on the Western Front
summary: In line with the 2019 film 1917, a quiet, unsettling, and grinding sort of grace is given to a time of misery and horror—it’s a hard thing to examine this period of history with anything less than disgust, and yet within it there are devastating human stories which must be heard and seen. All Quiet on the Western Front, a German novel which shed light on the horrors of the war for the average person, is brought to life in another form with this film, and it’s well worth the watch.

title: American Psycho
summary: In a similar camp as Fight Club, many people don’t truly understand where this film intends to go—they see it as a glorification of various tropes and behaviors, while in truth it is true satire and commentary through and through. While hyperbolic, it still digs deep at the fragility of minds and masculinity, and how our society has driven deep the wedge between reason and psychosis.

title: Annihilation
summary: A genuinely exciting ride which keeps its ultimate conclusion a well guarded secret, if nothing else. There’s a lot to unpack, and it’s up to the viewer whether the ending is worth the weight. Strong acting chops and good chemistry do wonders in this film.

title: Argo
summary: A thrilling and tense dive into the story of Americans being evacuated from Iran after the revolution, it has all the right pacing, gravity, and intensity that one could want for a thriller. While a fictitious take on the real thing, it still stands as a fantastic watch.

title: Armageddon
summary: This is a film that is shameless in owning what it is: a wild time through and through. If you want believability and grounded thrills, this isn’t for you. This is for the big kids who want to see space shuttles dodging asteroid chunks like the Falcon.

title: Army of the Dead
summary: A fairly disappointing film, but unfortunately quite typical of Zach Snyder’s signature style. Lots of loud screaming and action and explosions, but lacking in weight and heart.

title: Arrival
summary: A stand up thrill with some really mind twisting moments, but nothing that jumps out as a scare. It’s exactly what one could want with regards to building tension, however fantastical it may seem.

title: Assassin’s Creed
summary: When someone dares to step into the world of video game adaptations, they risk a great danger by failing to do it justice; video gamers are (to their detriment) fierce about their imagined ownership over IPs. This adaptation doesn’t really fall into the camp of rage inducing, but it’s not a really great film either.

title: Atlantis: The Lost Empire
summary: A classic adventure and a gem from the golden age of animation before the 3d, computer generated train picked up speed. Despite its fast pace it hits all the right notes and moments, and leaves you feeling good when you finish.

title: Atomic Blonde
summary: A film with complexity, depth, stunning visuals, out of this world action, and world class performances from a cast of exceptional actors–Atomic Blonde hits hard and doesn’t stop. This is a film that demands your attention to get the most out of it, but it’s well worth the dedicated attention.

title: Attack the Block
summary: Attack the Block is a wild ride, one that keeps moving and doesn’t stop. While Star Wars held John Boyega back with sloppy writing and a lack of development, he’s got free room to shine here, and he runs the shows. The cast might be younger, but they are well cast and full of the energy this film demands.

title: Avatar
summary: Avatar is, in essence, a glorified retelling of Pocahontas, and while it certainly sells itself with stunning visuals and a remarkably dense and well developed world, the comparisons are impossible to ignore, and because of that it’s easy to dismiss the film as a bit of a waste. Isolated and looked at independently, though, it hits hard with a filling plot and chemistry between characters that is spot on.

title: Avatar: Way of Water
summary: Of course the CGI is on another level, and the action is pounding, and the views are gorgeous, but it’s a tired story that isn’t good for much more than just background entertainment. It hardly pushes any frontiers on new stories or concepts, but it is a technical marvel that should be given its due.

title: Avatar: Fire and Ash
summary: See the review for The Way of Water, because it’s the exact same thing.

title: Awake
summary: A very interesting concept with rock solid performances carrying through to the end. It’s grounded, focusing closely on one family during the crisis, with compelling and believable interactions that really sell the gravity of the crisis.

title: A.X.L
summary: God awful. Just don’t watch it.

title: Baby Driver
summary: A fantastic thrill from start to finish, Baby Driver is a joyride through and through, with great driving scenes and meaningful tension that builds and builds and builds as the stakes get higher. A well rounded cast brings this story to life, and it couldn’t be better.

title: Band of Brothers
summary: One of the most deeply engaging narratives told of WWII, the show focuses on the individuals (all based on their real life counterparts) rather than the war at large; it navigates complex and deep (and often conflicting) human emotions tied to something that is genuinely inhumane by nature. The acting, the production design, and the empathetic approach to the subject matter all work in concert to make this one of the more important perspectives on WWII to view.

title: The Banshees of Inisherin
summary: Going into Banshees without any expectations or context is, perhaps, the wisest thing one can do; there’s no real way to sum up and prepare someone for what they’re about to watch, except to say that it will keep building and building and building until you’re chomping at the bit to know the end. It’s a wildly entertaining film, despite the unusual pacing.

title: Barbie
summary: Nothing less than an absolute triumph. A spectacular, hysterical, perfectly constructed masterpiece of modern societal critique, self-aware deprecation, and genuinely heartwarming affection. This is an instant and absolute success well worth the watch.

title: The Batman
summary: Given the misery of the DCEU Batman inclusions, The Batman is such a genuinely tremendous break from the mold and it hit every note it needed to. This take on DC’s iconic vigilante dives into what made Batman such an intriguing character: he was a detective solving crimes that the police just couldn’t. The film has all the gritty, dark, brooding and gothic elements one comes to expect from Batman, but the story and chemistry between actors is what really sells this film.

title: Battlestar Galactica
summary: 4 Seasons. Anyone who knows anything about me should know that Battlestar (the reimagined series) is bread and butter as far as I’m concerned. It’s got the suspense, the action, the science fiction, the politics, and the intrigue - and it has all of these in spades. BSG is a thrill from start to finish, even if the series took a few episodes to truly find itself.

title: Battle L.A.
summary: A fairly standard alien invasion story, it heats up faster than films like Independence Day, but still manages to set the stakes fairly high. It’s not the fight that is built up to in this, but rather the journey to the end of the fight that the characters are propelled towards. There are human moments for many of the characters, even if some of the acting might fall flat from time to time.

title: Beetlejuice
summary: A childhood classic (though not really for children children), Beetlejuice is as memorable as it is wild, with an outstanding performance by Keaton that will stick in your mind for years to come.

title: Better Call Saul
summary: On a second, complete watch through, it’s easy to fall in love with the show all over again. While the protagonist may be contemptible, Odenkirk is a great fit for the role, and his supporting cast members absolutely knock it out of the park. As a prequel to another tremendous series, Breaking Bad, Better Call Saul is a must-watch for anyone who's watched what came before.

title: Beyond Skyline
summary: A spectacularly disappointing sequel to a droll and uninspired blockbuster knock off. There’s a whole lot of cgi, but very little weight behind any of the big moments. It feels hollow, and made just for the effects.

title: Big Fish
summary: A tremendous thrill; wild and heart warming at the same time. Big Fish delivers on exploring what binds families together, and what can bridge gaps that divide people, something which often does happen in the strangest ways. The casting for Big Fish is one of its strongest elements, but the writing and eccentric nature of the story shouldn't be discounted.

title: Big Hero 6
summary: Decent enough for an animated film; it’s got some heartfelt moments and a fairly strong flow and vibe to it. Not a bad watch if you have kids.

title: The Big Short
summary: It’s no secret that just about every American has a great reason to despise the ultra-rich Wall Street executives; they’ve ruined much of this country with their greed. Movies like The Big Short do a great job giving the lay-person a look into that ‘other world’ and explain how things happened, but it can never truly capture the genuine disdain that many feel.

title: The Bike Riders
summary: A character-driven look at identity, belonging, and the slow evolution of a subculture. It’s more reflective than explosive. The performances carry the weight.

title: Bird Box
summary: A decent enough horror film that, albeit a tad absurd, does the bare minimum to keep the audience engaged through and through.

title: Blackberry
summary: A sharp, chaotic look at ambition, ego, and the kind of lightning-in-a-bottle success that rarely lasts. The performances carry a frantic energy that mirrors the rise and fall perfectly, even if the pacing occasionally sprints past moments worth sitting with. There’s a biting humor here that makes the collapse all the more satisfying.

title: Black Hawk Down
summary: Considered a landmark film to some, and just another Hollywood bungle to others, Black Hawk Down is a charged feature that throws the audience into the depths of war, showing all its most gruesome facets through a very human lens. Flaws aside, it’s an enjoyable watch with fast pacing.

title: Black Mass
summary: A grim and methodical descent into the kind of power that corrodes everything it touches. Depp’s performance is unsettling in a quiet, deliberate way, though the film itself at times feels more restrained than the story demands. Still, there’s an eerie weight to it that lingers.

title: Black Swan
summary: A mind bending narrative that unravels hand in hand with the depth of the protagonist’s growing madness. Drawing clear influence from Dostoevsky’s second novel, the plot is a one of a kind sort of adventure that can give even the least classic-inclined viewer a warm introduction to dance.

title: Blade: Trinity
summary: While many will argue that it’s the worst of the three, for me it’s one of the most thrilling and wild. With Trinity, they’ve taken a new approach to the humor and gone deeper with the tragic costs of Blade’s hunt than ever before. There’s plenty of action and pulp, and of course there’s always Snipes’ fantastic one liners.

title: Blade Runner
summary: An all time classic for science fiction, Blade Runner tells a story that others have toyed with before: man meets machine, and love complicates the situation. Blade Runner knows how to build the suspense, though, and how to establish a deeply rich world in which the story plays out. It can be a drag at times, but the payoff is well worth the investment.

title: Blade Runner 2049
summary: An example of a movie where the sequel is far stronger than the original, which is a considerable statement given the tremendous praise that should be given to the original Blade Runner of 1982. The introduction of modern filmmaking technology allows 2049 to go where Blade Runner never could, visually speaking, and strong writing keeps it gunning forwards at full speed.

title: The Blues Brothers
summary: A class that everyone should see at least five times. This has all the wit, humor, quirkiness, and Chicago history that one needs to enjoy a movie night - and the talented cast (with a tremendous ensemble of musical stars) delivers a performance you won’t forget.

title: Bombshell
summary: The understanding that much of the awful real world events inspiring the film were allowed to go on for so long is what really brings weight to this film–it’s merely a glance at the seedy and ugly underworld that reigns in the media industry. Like a horror film about a shark, it’s what’s not seen that’s even more terrifying. While a dramatization, Bombshell does a strong job of hammering the head of the nail.

title: Boogie Nights
summary: A film that strikes hard at some genuinely hysterical notions and narrative twists, it’s bogged down by a pace that demands much of its audience, and doesn’t always reward sufficiently enough. There are tremendously funny moments and the whole concept is rather bizarre, but still a fun watch.

title: The Brave Little Toaster
summary: An all time classic deserving of being rewatched, shared, and passed on through the generations.

title: Breaking Bad
summary: So much good to say, so hard to condense it into just a few sentences. Breaking Bad is an absolute ride through and through, with characters you’ll both love and hate, who give us a window into who many of us could very well become. It’s a human show that is also a strong commentary on the way our world is.

title: Bullet Train
summary: Over-hyped and poorly delivered; the concept is exciting, but the execution seemed messy and the narrative not wholly baked. It seems more to be a film composed of “what if we did...” moments instead of meaningful plot/character development beats.

title: Burn After Reading
summary: One of my top choices for any recommendations list for someone who wants humor served with their drama. The film is all about professional idiots who take their jobs way too seriously, and wind up being pulled deeper and deeper into a festering mess of epic proportions.

title: Cabin in the Woods
summary: A fantastic twist on the classic slasher/thriller, this is a movie that absolutely has to go on your Halloween or thriller list; it will hit all the right notes you’d want, and far exceeds many others you wouldn’t ever expect to find.

title: Captain Phillips
summary: A decent enough dramatization of the real world highjacking of a freighter, Tom Hanks rocks his role, as usual, though while it’s got a solid production behind it, Captain Philips drags in areas.

title: Casablanca
summary: Widely considered to be a timeless classic, Casablanca very much lives up to its reputation if one is fond of the slower paced, often overly-dramatized performances that were a custom of the time. A strong recommendation for any viewing night on the couch.

title: Cast Away
summary: An all time classic, Cast Away has some iconic lines, scenes, and images that are recognized even still. It’s a trying ordeal that Tom Hanks’ character pulls us through, but it’s a solid run which draws the audience in.

title: To Catch A Killer
summary: Not a terrible film, but also not one that’s overwhelmingly tremendous. It tries to push an unconventional hero type, and the circumstances are certainly gripping, but the follow up could be miles better.

title: Catch Me If You Can
summary: Catch Me If You Can is a delight, through and through. Tom Hanks and Leonardo DiCaprio strike up a memorable dynamic that plays out beautifully across the distance, weaving itself intricately to the looming point of collision.

title: Charlie Wilson’s War
summary: A surprisingly witty lens on a very serious chapter of geopolitical history. It balances charm and consequence well, though it occasionally glosses over the deeper ramifications of its own story. Still, it’s an engaging watch that leaves you thinking after the credits roll.

title: Charlie’s Angels
summary: Stylish, over-the-top, and fully aware of exactly what it is. It leans hard into spectacle and charm, sacrificing realism for pure entertainment, and honestly, it works. Not meant to be taken too seriously, and better for it.

title: Childhood 2.0
summary: A must-watch documentary for any parent, teacher, or childcare provider. It deals with the grim reality of how digital media is putting children in tremendous danger, and how things continue to spiral beyond control.

title: Chronicle
summary: Another film in the spirit of found footage, this one is an interesting take on the genre where the found footage isn’t recorded by accident, but rather intentionally. We see the characters marching boldly down a path from which there may be no return, and it’s a wild ride to see the paths each chooses.

title: Cloverfield
summary: A fairly strong contender for the films of the 2000s, this was a film that followed the “found footage” trend that exploded in the horror and supernatural horror genres. Full of constant motion and explosive jumps forward, many questions are posed and few are answered, leaving audiences wanting more.

title: 10 Cloverfield Lane
summary: Such a thrill. Genuinely and truly one of the more gripping Cloverfield films, it’s got such a fantastic take on the universe that’s been built, and leaves viewers just as anxious as the characters as it hurtles onwards towards the conclusion.

title: The Cloverfield Paradox
summary: There’s no denying that the film has twists and turns and a really engaging premise; it’s terrifying at times, mysterious, intriguing, and well worth the time spent watching it for the thrills and excitement. As a Cloverfield movie, though, it feels quite different from the two that came before it, with only one small connection to the franchise. It’s a great movie, but it doesn’t feel like Cloverfield.

title: Clue
summary: Perhaps one of the greatest films, Clue stands out as a hysterical, iconic classic that will have audiences clutching their sides with laughter. This is a film worth rewatching time and time again.

title: Code 8
summary: A decently interesting concept and a fairly grounded plot here make for an enjoyable time, but there’s a lot of shallow areas where the plot, and chemistry between characters, seems paper thin. Despite those flaws, it’s something new and interesting.

title: Contagion
summary: This is a film that keeps low to the ground, delivering on the tension surrounding what we’ve since learned is a very real, and very likely scenario. While it may have been hard to accept that Contagion was merely foreshadowing what would end up happening in real life, some of the predictions are unsettlingly accurate.

title: The Creator
summary: A fairly cliched story set in a fantastical, science fiction world, The Creator does a fairly good job of entertaining, though deep thought isn’t much required. It’s straight to the point, has beautiful visuals, and isn’t a terrible way to spend some time.

title: Darkest Hour
summary: A stunning performance from Gary Oldman, it’s yet another take on a very familiar subject for those aware of the history. Churchill’s reputation and the caricatures of him are world famous, and yet still, Oldman brings a new light to the man and shows the good, the bad, and the ugly, honoring the complexity of the time and the heroism of the man.

title: Dawn of the Dead
summary: A personal favorite specifically for the fact that it’s an easy watch—something explosive, witty, gorey, full of fulls, and it doesn’t take itself too seriously. A great cast make up with actors who play well off each other allows for a great ride from start to finish.

title: The Day After Tomorrow
summary: A staple of the 2000s that helped to set up a whole genre obsessed with end of the world disasters. Not a bad film by any means, but very much a thing of its time.

title: Man of Steel
summary: There’s a special kind of irritable pain experienced when watching the kick off of the DCEU; it’s founded in disappointment in visual effects, obnoxious fights, overdramatized bits and a plot that reads as “because it needs to happen”. There’s little nuance, limited space for genuine emotional investment from the audience, and Man of Steel’s most notable legacy is that it sets the stage for a franchise that reads like a kid playing with action figures. There’s very little sinew holding the muscle and bone together.

title: Batman V Superman: Dawn of Justice
summary: It is very difficult to find positive things to say about this film. Simply put, the second installment in the DCEU doubles down on all the things its predecessor did wrong. There is no sense of cohesiveness, and the plot remains a slapdash effort to churn out something that was not fully baked.

title: Suicide Squad
summary: Arguably the best of the three films in the DCEU thus far, it still doesn’t clear the bar for being “good”, unfortunately. Suicide Squad takes a much more light hearted approach to the DC universe, something sorely needed and entirely missing from the previous two films. In this movie, character backgrounds and motivations are explored and given weight and meaning, something which pushes the story forward, but isn’t enough to save the film.

title: Wonder Woman
summary: There are elements to Wonder Woman that bring a sense of optimism and hope to the DCEU, moments that posit interesting quandaries and exciting ideas; there are characters with genuine motivations that are explored; there are even some well crafted fight scenes. But ultimately, Wonder Woman struggles with many of the same problems that prior installments of the DCEU have hit rock bottom with: needless and bizarrely choreographed fights, dismal graphic effects, and plot developments that seem either lackluster or down right out of place.

title: Justice League
summary: There are no words to express just how terrible this movie is.

title: Aquaman
summary: Feeling the fatigue of having to sit through these terrible movies, Aquaman simply slid past without flare or fascination. It was slow enough and unengaging that it really could only be viewed in small bursts, rather than watching it all at once—something very unusual for me. Not terrible, but not good.

title: Shazam!
summary: Quirky, weird, and quite lighthearted compared to the rest of the DCEU, it does a few things new that stick the landing, and it certainly exists in its own pocket where it doesn’t rely on the rest of the franchise. This is a major strength. It’s certainly not a home run, but it’s a far cry better than the rest of this miserable franchise.

title: Joker
summary: While this could have been a standout set against the backdrop of a floundering franchise, Joker leaned heavily into the extreme and attempted to portray a villain in a light other than what he deserves. The glorification of deficiencies in mental wellbeing doesn’t sell; the Joker is a fantastic villain when he’s left to his hijinks, but trying to tell a grounded and gritty, emotional rise to infamy where its based in a very real world just glorifies all the wrong parts.

title: Death of a Unicorn
summary: Absurdism at its peak, Death of a Unicorn is a perfect medley of serious acting, punchy beats, the right amount of satire, and a concept so bizarre that you’ll never see it coming, even if it tells you exactly what will happen.

title: The Departed
summary: When it comes to a non-stop ride of epic excitement and unexpected turns, The Departed is a must-see. There are few films which combine narcissists, rats, and people just trying to survive as well as this.

title: The Diplomat
summary: When the main complaint I can make is that the linchpin for the entire show and the catalyst for the whole plot (an attack on a naval vessel) seemed implausible, and everything else was an absolute thrill, you can be assured it’s a great show to get into. The complexity and layering to the mysteries explored is incredibly well designed, and the series flies by faster than one might want. There’s high hopes for where it goes next.

title: District 9
summary: District 9 is a fantastic foray into the sci-fi genre, with a ground narrative that is accompanied by outrageous and exciting moments that will catch audiences by surprise. While far-fetched, the film plays itself off as if it’s an everyday sort of occurrence, focusing on the human conflicts rather than the spectacle of alien arrival on Earth.

title: Doctor Sleep
summary: An absolute triumph. Mike Flanagan once again delivers a stunning masterpiece, this time another adaptation that not only brings another of Stephen King’s books to life, but also pays dutiful homage to The Shining itself. An absolute thrill to watch.

title: Don’t Look Up
summary: A decent enough film with a grim but honest—and hyperbolic—look at how our society works today. Things suck, for sure, but they’re only going to get worse, and Don’t Look Up certainly points that out.

title: Downfall
summary: A film that has quite a reputation for its poignancy, it depicts the most villainous man known to history in a way that doesn’t humanize him, but does give insights into the collapse of his life and his world. There is no sympathy for the man, nor his supporters, but it is a thing of fascination to see how one man’s mind can unravel in an instant when reality defies desire.

title: Dredd
summary: Pure pulp, pure absurdity, and pure enjoyment. Karl Urban is a solid choice for this iconic character’s reboot, and while it definitely hits on the wild and unhinged premise of the character, it does so with style that shrugs off any shackles of realism and presents a film that can simply be enjoyed for what it is.

title: Drive
summary: A film with this sort of a plot needs to be heavy hitting and loud, but instead we got a very tame, very slow, very quiet sort of build up with no strong climax or ending. In the same genre, one is much better off watching Baby Driver or any of the Statham Transporter films.

title: Dune
summary: A phenomenal adaptation that knows when to break from the source material and do its own thing. While unique in its own ways, Dune pays homage to the adaptations that came before, but while owning its own identity and establishing a clear path forward.

title: Dune: Part 2
summary: What may well be an all time favorite and top five, Dune: Part 2 is a stunning, stupefying smash hit that delivers on all that was promised and more. Everything builds to a crescendo of remarkable passion and thrill; it’s impossible to imagine this being any more incredible than it already is.

title: Dunkirk
summary: A unique take on a monumental moment in human and military history: the evacuation of some 300,000 men from the shores of Europe. Told from three perspectives, the film takes a unique approach in not once showing the enemy, something which generates a sense of unease, discomfort, and anxiousness, a fitting vibe given the nature of the story. A true thrill, through and through.

title: Edge of Tomorrow
summary: Genuinely one of the most exciting and riveting takes on sci-fi alien invasions that has come out thus far in the 21st century. There is nothing slowing down this beast of a film, and between the fantastic dynamic between characters, the exciting and brisk pace of the narrative, the design of the creatures, and execution of the combat, you’re in for a fantastic ride.

title: El Camino
summary: Something that–arguably–did not need to be made - it takes the reader on a journey to follow the close of a chapter that was already closed, meaning it has to be reopened first. That in itself is where the problems start. El Camino is that trope in which everything in fantasy or sci-fi has to be over-explained. Audience members can be content watching the finale of Breaking Bad without feeling any need to watch this, but it isn’t entirely a waste of time, either.

title: Elysium
summary: A cliche spun into a grander production, the narrative doesn’t bring anything new to the “man trying to survive in the dystopian society of inequality” genre, but it does have some delightful scenes and decent acting. It’s not a disappointment, but certainly not anything grand and new.

title: Enemy at the Gates
summary: Average. It’s got decent enough tension, but the constant hopping around makes it a bore and almost exhausting to follow, with the fast bits seeming slow, and the things we want slowed down being far too fast.

title: Escape from L.A.
summary: A campy, exaggerated sequel that leans fully into its own ridiculousness. It lacks the edge of its predecessor but makes up for it with sheer audacity and style. Not refined, but undeniably entertaining in its own strange way.

title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
summary: A film so bizarre, so tremendous, and so spectacular that there are few words to truly describe it. A beautiful and highly talented cast has come together to create a deeply compelling, abstract portrait of a family that, had Hemingway been responsible for it, could have been told in a short story of five hundred words. But instead, it has been spun into a tapestry that defies all expectations, and leaves the viewer fulfilled and bewildered.

title: Ex Machina
summary: There are few films that will generate such a thrill, and keep an audience so captivated as Ex Machina. It’s a stunningly well crafted work of art that blends so many elements and genres, marrying horror and suspense with science fiction and reality. It will keep any audience at the edge of their seat.

title: The Exorcist
summary: No one can deny that The Exorcist has secured a golden place in the realm of film for its impact and uniqueness at the time of its release. It blazes a new trail, but compared to horror and supernatural thrillers made today, it’s pacing feels far too slow to trailblaze in any which direction. While by no means a bad film, for someone who didn’t grow up with it before all those that followed, it doesn’t carry the same impact.

title: Extinction
summary: Just terrible, downright. An interesting concept for sure, but terribly executed on all accounts.

title: Extraction
summary: A new take on the non-stop action thriller genre, Extraction hits all the notes you’d want, and expect, but with new twists and approaches to old tropes. It’s packed full of epic fights and non-stop movement, and sure to keep anyone entertained.

title: Extraction 2
summary: Continuing after the events of the first film, Extraction 2 delivers on the same promises made in the first: non-stop action, bigger stakes, bigger stunts, and now an attempt at layering new emotional beats that, while surprising to see, definitely help on the delivery of all the rest.

title: The Fabelmans
summary: Spielberg strikes off towards home in a very personal, very candid self portrait of his own family and youth, and while some of it is no doubt wrapped and tailored with the skilled hands of a story teller, there’s undeniable heart at the core of this film. There’s no such thing as true closure, but through The Fabelmans we as the audience are able to make peace with a number of things and accept the way they are.

title: Fahrenheit 451
summary: As far as heartless, lackluster adaptations that break hard from the source material, this one is pretty criminal, and misses much of the original meaning. But, as far as entertainment, people who care little about the book may be content and occupied without complaint for a few hours. It’s mindless, and that’s the problem.

title: Fall of the House of Usher
summary: This is undoubtedly the crowning achievement of Mike Flanagan’s reign of terror; it’s an amalgamation of the works of Edgar Allan Poe sewn together with threads of sheer terror and gruesome deaths that you can feel approaching, but will never be prepared for.

title: The Fast and the Furious
summary: The only thing faster than the cars is the speed at which the plot slams into the concrete. This is, in essence, Need for Speed - if it became a franchise of films: lots of speed and drama and big things, but no tight narrative. It’s entertaining for what it is, but suspension of disbelief is essential.

title: 2 Fast 2 Furious
summary: It’s faster than the first, but still campy enough to keep it in the same bracket of quality. The effects, stunts, and momentum are better, though 2 Fast 2 Furious clings to the same elements that dragged down the first movie - too high-flying to make complete sense or keep the wheels on the road.

title: Fast & Furious
summary: A deeper, more nuanced plot which dives into complex relationships in ways the first two films never could. The stakes are higher largely because there is a great deal which has already been established; the audience is able to sink their teeth into the narrative rather than trying to buy into the universe. A much needed improvement to the franchise.

title: Fast Five
summary: It’s with Fast Five, if you’re watching in chronological order, that investment in the whole story of this franchise starts to really pay off. Threads established earlier on are pulled once more, and characters are really given the space needed to deliver on the true potential of the franchise. While more tight knit and smooth through and through, Fast Five is also where things start to branch into the bizarre.

title: Fast & Furious 6
summary: The thrills and excitement continue on with Fast & Furious 6, though the bizarre hijinks seem to take on a life of their own. The further in on the plot this franchise goes, the more absurd the lengths to which the characters will go, to the point that now, in the 6th film one has to completely suspend disbelief.

title: The Fast and the Furious: Tokyo Drift
summary: It’s just bad; plain and simple. Flat, uninspired acting, hard-to-believe casting, weak character motivations, paper thin logic... it’s a mess from top to bottom.

title: Furious 7
summary: Furious 7 was god awful. The narrative jumps all around with little cohesion, and a lot of things feel shoehorned in just to get an inclusion. It’s just a plain old mess.

title: Fear Street Part 1: 1994
summary: A thrilling, fast past horror flick that draws back to what makes horror films so genuinely delightful. Fear Street Part 1 is the first of three expertly made films that tell a wild, unchained story that fits together like the last few pieces of a puzzle. Spectacularly satisfying, thoroughly thrillingly, and absolutely worth the watch.

title: Fear Street Part 2: 1978
summary: The second part of the Fear Street trilogy, it’s just as engaging and horrific as the first. Blood, gore, and all sorts of mess captivate while the quick narrative movements and compelling character relationships draw the audience to the edge of their seat. This series only gets better the deeper in you go.

title: Fear Street Part 3: 1666
summary: A climactic ending to a fantastic trilogy, Fear Street Part 3 finishes strong, and ties it all together with a perfect, bloody bow on top. There is little room for critique when it’s this damn good.

title: Fear Street Part: Prom Queen
summary: This is clear proof that Fear Street is not slowing down. Set in the same universe as the prior films, it breaks off to do its own thing and sticks the landing. An instant contender for some of the best slashers around.

title: Fight Club
summary: A film that’s become the center of endless discourse and analysis, it’s deserving of that attention, but with flaws of its own to balance out that fame. While not something that will leave someone yearning for rewatches, it’s still a plenty enjoyable film with an unraveling that is well paced and fairly well delivered.

title: Freaky
summary: An intriguing take on Freaky Friday, where two people swap bodies, it’s made more thrilling with a bit of murder being thrown into the mix. While bizarre and laughable at times, it certainly sticks to its vibes and is a good watch for any movie night.

title: Freaky Friday
summary: Nothing needs to be said about Freaky Friday except that it’s an all time classic full of laughs and thrills. You can’t go wrong with this as your movie night choice.

title: Free Fire
summary: Engaging and unique in its take on a knife fight in a phone booth trope, Free Fire is a neat concept that at times can be a bit slow and dry, but otherwise finds ways to keep the audience guessing as to what will unfold next. It demands a close eye and constant attention, but all the plots are tied up by the final scenes.

title: The Fugitive
summary: It’s Harrison Ford at his best - pointing at everyone, shouting constantly, and trying to figure out who murdered his wife. There’s great twists and layering to this classic thriller, and the interactions between the key stars is well worth sitting down for the watch. This is a movie you can’t go wrong choosing.

title: Fury
summary: There’s something you come to expect when you hear the name Brad Pitt; him in a deeply serious role that depicts the growing strain of a soldier in war is not the first thing to come to my mind, however. Fury is a great delve into the horrors and complex personal relationships that dominated World War II, where the mental and emotional strain of combat and the conflict of morals were just as dangerous as the fighting.

title: Galaxy Quest
summary: The best and most wild adventure through science fiction available on your screen. It has the cockiness of Space Balls, the deep nuance of Star Trek, and the fast flying delight of Star Wars; Galaxy Quest is the ultimate letter to fans and creators alike, and it does everything right to sell the audience that it is the absolute authority on parodying the real world we try so hard to escape.

title: Game Night
summary: A genuine thrill. Game Night is a fantastic watch for any sort of occasion, with tremendous wit and humor, cleverness, stellar chemistry between actors, and a genuinely solid plot through and through. This film definitely takes top prize.

title: Geostorm
summary: Pretty damn fucking bad.

title: Gladiator
summary: Gladiator is a journey through and through, one that draws upon themes of resilience, justice, and determination. Russell Crowe is a fantastic choice for the role, one which he brings a sense of emotional depth too that might have initially been unexpected. The production is of a prime quality, and the dynamics which evolve between characters do much to cement this as a classic watch.

title: Gladiator II
summary: A fantastic sequel, deserving of the legacy it holds, and full of outstanding thrills and powerful moments. Denzel shines here, as he often does in his work; it’s good material, good writing, and fantastic acting and production quality.

title: Godzilla Minus One
summary: When the Monsterverse franchise began churning out Godzilla movies like there was no tomorrow, it offered a chance for a new take on a classic, but so much of that franchise focuses on the wild fights and CGI rather than the people caught in the middle. Godzilla Minus One is a welcome return to form that looks splendid, but may seem a bit hamfisted at times.

title: Gran Turismo
summary: Another entry into the “zero to hero” genre of films, Gran Turismo stands out as one that’s fast paced, driven by an emotional core, and with genuine chemistry between the cast that will demand your focus. It’s an especially decent watch when taking into account the good that it does by highlighting the genuine power of the underdog.

title: The Gray Man
summary: A fairly decent action film with a solid choice for a leading man, there’s a lot of potential for the further serialization of the franchise in film, but it doesn’t exactly add anything new to the genre. A decent watch for good action, all the same.

title: Grease
summary: A much loved classic, the dialogue, music, aesthetic, and themes are certainly a mark of the film’s time, but one that is charming and endearing all the same.

title: Greenland
summary: Decent enough as far as disaster films go; it’s got all the typical explosive action you’d expect, the gripping tension, the dramatic bonds of family and love. Standard, loud, exciting, but not all too deep.

title: Greenland 2: Migration
summary: More grounded than most disaster films, focusing less on spectacle and more on the human scramble to survive. It’s tense in a quieter, more personal way, even if some beats feel familiar. The restraint works in its favor.

title: Green Zone
summary: A pretty dang solid movie through and through, touching on parts of American history that are still very controversial in the eyes of many.

title: Greyhound
summary: While some moments are tense and thrilling, Greyhound fails to deliver on the great potential it advertised. Tom Hanks is a convincing commander, but the stitching of the narrative and the visual language used to guide the audience through the plot is a leaky boat at best.

title: Death Proof
summary: Death Proof is one of those Tarantino films that makes a bright red wet spot on the pavement that’s hard to scrub away. It’s flashy and violent and dramatic in ways that are almost unique to Tarantino; it doesn’t follow the model you’d expect, and is outlandish at times with its boisterous characters, but it is a thrill if nothing else.

title: Groundhog Day
summary: Witty, relatable, and certainly of its time—Groundhog day delivers a comfortable and reliable comedy for the season, one that you won’t much mind watching again, and again, and again, and again, year after year.

title: Hail, Caesar
summary: This is one of those movies that is absurd, outlandish, bizarre, and entirely delightful through and through. Determination and foolishness balance each other out as characters play off one another and race towards an unanticipated conclusion that will bring you back for rewatches time and time again.

title: The Haunting of Hill House
summary: One of Mike Flanagan’s works, this show will leave you reeling with each episode as you follow the story of a family that is coming apart at the seams with one common factor tearing it apart—the Hill House. There’s startling scares and unsettling undertones that will demand your attention, and leave you eager for a rewatch.

title: The Haunting of Bly Manor
summary: A genuinely thrilling horror show that earns its place. One of Mike Flanagan’s works, it fits into a new canon of horror designed by a man who has a clear understanding of what can unsettle and disturb. It isn’t the jump scares in this that will get you—it’s the anticipation of how it all unravels that is going to leave you on the edge of your seat.

title: Heat
summary: Heat is the type of film that almost makes you root for the villains, characters who have depth and reasonable motives that a slight twisting of morals could almost justify. Al Pacino and Robert De Niro bring to the forefront the unrestrained power which propels both characters on to a climactic and explosive confrontation that is well worth the investment of time.

title: Heretic
summary: An interesting concept that fell flat with a serious lack of coherence in the third act, with lackluster twists and things that just didn’t quite add up. What serves as a good initial building of tension is spoiled by a failure to follow through. Great performances are wasted on poor writing.

title: Hitch
summary: Hitch is a classic of the 2000s that became an instant hit, and certainly held its weight as one of the last great romance comedies.

title: Hocus Pocus
summary: A delightful classic that has always been close to home; high flying and wild, with thrills for the whole family.

title: House of the Dragon
summary: House of the Dragon is an ambitious, albeit desperate attempt to rekindle the flame ignited by Game of Thrones. While there is certainly the same sex and violence that made the original series so epic, House of the Dragon hyper focuses its lens on one family, rather than many. It lacks the breadth of the original, but still finds a way to make the drama thrilling and engaging.

title: A House of Dynamite
summary: An apocalyptic political thriller, A House of Dynamite delves into the heart of the human experiences around a devastating nuclear attack, and while it does a tremendous job of raising the heat incrementally, the lack of any concrete resolution leaves it feeling sorely incomplete.

title: How It Ends
summary: A film that does a decent job of building and sustaining tension, though some bits of the film just don’t quite fit together. While the intrigue of the disaster will keep some hooked, the ending doesn’t quite seem worth the wait.

title: The Hunger Games
summary: A solid introduction to a world built on spectacle and control. It balances action with underlying commentary effectively. A strong starting point for the series.

title: The Hunger Games: Catching Fire
summary: Builds on the first in nearly every way, raising both stakes and tension. There’s a growing sense of unrest that gives the story real momentum. Arguably the strongest entry.

title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay (Part 1)
summary: More subdued, focusing on propaganda and psychological toll rather than action. It’s slower, but intentionally so. A necessary bridge, even if not the most exciting.

title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay (Part 2)
summary: A darker, more somber conclusion that leans into the cost of rebellion. It doesn’t shy away from the consequences. A fitting, if heavy, end.

title: The Hurt Locker
summary: A tense, intimate look at the psychological strain of war. It’s less about conflict and more about the individuals within it. Quietly powerful.

title: In the Heights
summary: Vibrant, heartfelt, and full of life, with music that carries both joy and longing. It celebrates community while still acknowledging struggle in a meaningful way. There’s a sincerity here that’s hard not to appreciate.

title: Inception
summary: Mind bending and thrilling, Inception is both iconic and historic in its impact, depth, and ingenuity both of narrative exploration and cinematic technical achievement. One of Nolan’s best works so far.

title: Independence Day
summary: A classic holiday blockbuster, Independence Day delivers on the thrills you want without the complexity common amongst other films in this genre; it doesn’t try to make itself something it’s not—it owns its place and hits its marks all the way through.

title: Independence Day: Resurgence
summary: Even the director himself said it shouldn’t have been made. Painful from the beginning all the way through to the end.

title: Ink
summary: A striking narrative that delves deep into the complex relationship between a father and his child that is jeopardized by a compulsive obsession with work. Emotionally resonant and deeply moving, the combination of its score, imagery, and well knit narrative makes for a tremendous experience of film.

title: Inside Man
summary: A film that didn’t hit quite as hard the second time around, but is still an exciting thrill from the get-go. Denzel Washington does a fantastic job of making his character someone the audience will want to stay close to, but many of the other components of the film are not so well executed, leaving much of the twist to be revealed at the end in a way that doesn’t exactly feel earned. The first time watching is an absolute thrill; the second time, it starts to feel like being held hostage in a bank.

title: Insidious
summary: You’re going to get exactly what you’d expect from Insidious; a few good jump scares, some unsettling concepts, and some characters that are just too stupid for their own good. It’s a decent enough film, but one that doesn’t do anything spectacular or new.

title: Into the Storm
summary: Into the Storm has a thrilling premise and nonstop action, which works well considering that some of the characters fall flat or are just not well suited to the actors that were cast. The film relies heavily on a few of its players to carry the film, but there are some cool moments and very well constructed graphics and the destruction is tremendously well assembled.

title: It
summary: Unsettling is the perfect term to describe the 2017 remake of Steven King’s It; it’s not scary so much as unnerving, and that’s exactly how horror like this should be. Viewers are given a very good reason to be afraid of Pennywise the Clown, but it’s really the depth of relationships between the young cast members that hits such a high note and makes this film very much worth the watch.

title: The Hunt for Red October
summary: The Hunt for Red October is a stellar adaptation, and builds suspense through to the end where all the waiting pays off. Perfectly cast actors bring to life believable, well crafted characters who demand the audience’s reaction, for better or worse, as they weave their way through the plot. The story unravels at a steady pace, pulling the audience down into the depths along with the ship.

title: Jack Reacher: Never Go Back
summary: A fairly standard action flick; it’s got the star power and it has the punch, but some of the scenes while well choreographed definitely lean into the absurd and highly unlikely, something which, at times, is hard to look past when following the story.

title: Dr. No
summary: Through the lens of taking it for what it was at the time, Dr. No stands out as a leap into a wild genre that draws on many tropes and paints a strong picture of what was the ideal specimen of the time. Through a modern lens from one who prefers smooth, nuanced writing and production, it’s a difficult thing to sit through all the way, with many leaps and gaps left for viewers to stumble across. Important for what it was, but there are better films to watch.

title: From Russia with Love
summary: It’s a difficult thing for me to put my finger on specifically, but there’s something about From Russia With Love that just failed to hit its mark. A cunning and educated reviewer could easily manifest a thorough analysis worthy of the film’s renown, but in my words it just felt boring, uninteresting, and terribly exhausting.

title: Goldfinger
summary: A complete turn around from the previous film, From Russia With Love, Goldfinger hits the spot and keeps on going, despite some of the tropes which haven’t aged quite well. The villain has a quirky and wild plan, James is at his horniest, and the gadgets punctuate the film with the right amount of spice and wit that one should come to expect from a Bond film.

title: John Wick
summary: A new entry into a crowded field, John Wick pulls off what a lot of other action movies struggle to do: focus on what works—the fights. Where movie franchises like the Transporter and Bourne try to build out a more layered plot with twists along the way, John Wick tells a straightforward story that lets you enjoy what you’re there to see, violence.

title: John Wick: Chapter 2
summary: A very strong follow up to the original; well worth the watch!

title: From the World of John Wick: Ballerina
summary: Exactly what you’d expect from John Wick—violent. Fairly solid all around.

title: Jurassic Park
summary: An iconic classic that has stood the test of time, at least thus far. The concept, while bizarre, is one that speaks to a childhood love of science and adventure, and an imagination that runs just as wild as the creatures on screen. Jurassic Park is a bold venture forward and well worth the watch.

title: Jurassic World
summary: A fairly standard reboot that is similar to many other attempts to revive classic franchises. This one draws on star power and higher stakes, but also pays good homage to the spirit of the original, where hubris and arrogance and ulterior motives and villainy weave together a disaster of epic proportions.

title: Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom
summary: Jurassic World’s sequel provides the same sort of ride as its predecessor—it will keep you entertained, but it’s terribly shallow when it comes to depth of plot.

title: Kick-Ass
summary: Epic. A standout ride for thrills and excitement with fun twists and delightful chemistry between the leading actors.

title: The King
summary: A really curious adventure of young Henry V as he comes into his own, there’s a lot of really grounded performances, but the passion is so quiet that it doesn’t ring as the soaring tale often ascribed to Henry in the history books. Timothy Chalamet does a great job embodying one portrayal of the king that I don’t believe has been seen before.

title: Kingdom of Heaven
summary: This film wanders a long road, one filled with deep moments between characters that drive home just what is at stake, and why the characters are motivated in the way that they are. This film is grand in scope and tremendous in its execution, but it’s an important note to make that the director’s cut is the only version of this film you should watch.

title: Kiss Kiss Bang Bang
summary: A tremendous and delightful narrative, expertly weaved, that unravels bit by bit the deeper down the rabbit hole the audience goes. The chemistry between cast members, along with the sometimes-bizarre approach to narration, make for a genuinely fun and exciting time, and the fast paced movement keeps the audience from forgetting that there is a quite serious plot buried underneath.

title: Knightfall
summary: A fairly decent adventure through the templar Knights of old—the drama builds at a decent pace and keeps going full steam ahead. The scope and scale of the production is fairly solid, but could always build to something stronger.

title: Knives Out
summary: The first of a rather wild and unconventional series, Rian Johnson does a fantastic job of crafting a brilliant script, with an all star cast leading it to the forefront of mystery films in the same vein as the all time classic CLUE. This is a sure fire hit, and well worth future rewatches.

title: Glass Onion
summary: Lightning strikes twice for Rian Johnson with this second installment in the wild adventures of Detective Benoit Blanc. The fast paced mystery plot keeps audiences glued and eager for the reveals, something they’ll be trying to discern for themselves and arguing with fellow moviegoers over their hunches.

title: Knowing
summary: An intriguing premise that gradually leans further into existential territory. It’s uneven at times, but there’s something compelling about its willingness to go where it does. Not perfect, but memorable.

title: L.A. Confidential
summary: A solid and true choice for an entertaining thriller that borders on the edge of comedy, L.A. Confidential is a strong contender for any movie night for its scope, scale, and depth alone, not to mention the all-star cast who knock it way out of the park. There are good twists, well organized layers, and iconic and memorable lines.

title: Land of the Dead
summary: You get what you pay for when it comes to watching Land of the Dead. It’s nowhere near as thrilling as some of the others, and it branches off into a new direction that tests the limits of what fits within the broadly accepted genre of zombie behavior. Regardless, there is lots of blood.

title: Last Night in Soho
summary: A tremendous and thrilling ride through psychological twists and terrors that will leave viewers clawing for answers as the mystery plot thickens, and then finally unravels at the end. Subsequent rewatches are sure to reveal new layers not seen the first time. It’s a solid choice for any movie night.

title: The Last Samurai
summary: One of my all time favorites, The Last Samurai boldly rides forth in the spirit of James Clavell’s classic novel SHOGUN–though not directly linked to it–drawing upon many of the same concepts of foreign eyes viewing Japanese culture through a lens of preconceptions that are terribly wrong. Tom Cruise and Ken Watanabe shine in this film; this is a must watch.

title: Leave the World Behind
summary: A movie that unsettles more than terrifies—it delivers for the audience a great volume of questions but little to direct them in any one direction. If art is meant to inspire thinking, Leave the World Behind certainly achieves that goal; whether that’s a positive or not is up for debate.

title: The Legend of Zorro
summary: The Legend of Zorro is one of those Hollywood hits that has a sweet spot in the memories of many, with iconic lines and heart-swelling action and passion. It’s campy and corny at times, but wholly entertaining and thrilling. This is a film that will welcome you home no matter how many years have passed.

title: Looper
summary: A genuine thrill. This film goes places you’ll expect, but never see coming all the same. It’s a thrilling, exciting, and worthwhile watch that will be equally pleasing the second and third time around.

title: Love and Monsters
summary: Quirky and somewhat absurd at times, it has enough heart to be charming and just enough drama to be engaging.

title: Madam Secretary
summary: On par with the West Wing and The Newsroom with its charming and optimistic takes, it’s nonetheless a delight to watch—one which will give hope in a bleak and trying time.

title: Mad Max: Fury Road
summary: Action packed fuel courses through this film, and it’s an absolute ride of a time. If there’s ever a go-to film to get your blood pumping, it’s Mad Max: Fury Road.

title: Mamma Mia!
summary: A joyful ride that hits all the right vibes. While it certainly doesn’t reach the heights of its sequel, Mamma Mia! is still a tremendous thrill and well worth the watch.

title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again
summary: A joy. A thrill. The best pick to watch on a day that’s got you down; it’s a delight.

title: The Man in the High Castle
summary: A chilling alternate history that explores power and control in unsettling ways. It’s slow at times, but the world-building is strong. There’s a constant sense of unease.

title: Margin Call
summary: A quiet, almost clinical dissection of a financial system on the brink, where the real tension comes not from action, but from decisions made in sterile rooms with massive consequences. The film doesn’t try to over-explain itself, instead trusting the weight of what’s happening—and the people navigating it—to carry the story. There’s something deeply unsettling in how calm it all feels.

title: The Martian
summary: The Martian is an absolutely tremendous film. It’s thrilling and delightful and comedic and emotional, all at the right moments when it needs to be. Matt Damon delivers a stunning performance worthy of acclaim. Rewatch after rewatch, it’s a thrill through and through.

title: Captain America: The First Avenger
summary: Soon to be added.

title: Captain Marvel
summary: Soon to be added.

title: Iron Man
summary: The one that started it all—Iron Man strikes a chord of nostalgia for a lot of people, and while some of it may be more of a feeling of the times rather than the quality of the film, the movie itself still offers good laughs and a warm sense of homecoming. As the film that officially kicked off the MCU, it’s considered essential and impervious to criticism, at least online.

title: Loki
summary: A stellar start to a new adventure; thematically, this should have been the first entry into the new phase, with the multiverse being the central theme to it all. Regardless, it’s well worth the watch, and offers a fresh breath of air for a character that has otherwise seemed to find a conclusion to his arc.

title: What If...?
summary: Soon to be added.

title: WandaVision
summary: Steady and curious, WandaVision is perhaps the most unique thing to come to the MCU since its inception. While it certainly kept viewers on their toes and stoked rampant speculation, it seemed to pay off on the leads just as efficiently as it introduced them, and that’s a great thing.

title: Deadpool & Wolverine
summary: If vulgarity, gore, and complete bullshit and rowdiness are your vibe, you’re going to love this movie. It draws upon the same stupid magic of the first two Deadpool movies and gives you exactly what it is you wanted. If it’s not your bag, it won’t hit the right notes, but for all of us degenerates it’s a wild time.

title: Eternals
summary: Just exhausting and dull. So much potential, but for a two and a half hour movie, it still never found its direction or footing. A very big let down, albeit one with stunning visuals.

title: Falcon & the Winter Soldier
