title: Werewolf by Night
summary: Not a terrible adventure to embark upon, especially if one has a taste for the classics of Hollywood. While certainly a product of modern cinema, the film plays well into classic tropes with strong beats of movement and action, though the plot is without any significant twists, and has many choices which feel like very safe moves.

title: Guardians of the Galaxy: Holiday Special
summary: Soon to be added.

title: Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania
summary: A disappointing entry into the MCU, Ant-Man falls flat where once it held its own. What is meant to be a grand venture into the multiverse itself, Ant-Man is a drawn out, winding story with beats that don’t land and many moments that just didn’t click. It has the big bang the MCU is known for, but it’s far from being worthy.

title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
summary: Surprisingly the weakest film in the GoTG Trilogy from James Gunn, this film felt as if there was too much packed in and not enough time spent on each of the various threads. It’s clear what the intention was, and some of the heart is definitely there, but overall the film fell flat and failed to deliver on the comedic punches that the prior two had in such spades.

title: Secret Invasion
summary: Thoroughly disappointing; little more to say.

title: The Marvels
summary: Soon to be added.

title: Agatha: Coven of Chaos
summary: Soon to be added.

title: Captain America: New World Order
summary: Soon to be added.

title: Daredevil: Born Again
summary: Soon to be added.

title: X-Men 97
summary: Soon to be added.

title: Thunderbolts*: “The New Avengers”
summary: A fun new chapter that lands on steady feet in comparison to the other entries into the 4th and 5th phases of the MCU. In this, we’re not bound to the past, but rather building forwards for a new set of arcs.

title: Ironheart
summary: Soon to be added.

title: The Fantastic Four: First Steps
summary: A decent enough ride with a surprisingly short run time; it meets expectations, but doesn’t offer the same thrills and excitement as prior entries in the franchise.

title: The Mask of Zorro
summary: A joy. A thrill. A delightful adventure full of the tension and romance and heroics and revenge that makes any story a genuine pleasure. Antonio Banderas and Catherine Zeta-Jones charm and seduce the audience, and everything falls into place beside that.

title: Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World
summary: The film is stunning, fantastic, and a tremendous thrill to watch, even if it has a slower pace than what might be expected. It draws heavily on source material from the books, but by picking and choosing which elements are carried over, the film sails on to greater heights. An absolute must watch.

title: The Matrix
summary: Something wild, deep, and tremendously entertaining–The Matrix kicks off a stellar trilogy in which the audience is forced to contend with the question: are we truly in control of our own lives? It’s a question worth asking, and one that the film delves into with a brilliant twist of science fiction and philosophy, coupled with fast paced action that established the film as an instant classic.

title: The Matrix: Reloaded
summary: A strong addition to the story, Reloaded takes us deeper down the rabbit hole in pursuit of Alice, and the truth. The cast gets larger, the stakes are higher, and the action is still top notch, even if it does appear somewhat dated by today’s standards. An all around solid choice to watch.

title: Matrix: Revolutions
summary: A fitting conclusion to a stellar adventure, The Matrix: Revolutions ties things off in a way that many might not expect, and in ways some disagreed with, but all the same it was something which generated considerable discourse, and is worthy of its bookend status.

title: The Matrix Resurrections
summary: The Matrix Resurrections is a film that didn’t need to be made, but touches on the elements one would expect, and perhaps even want in an unneeded sequel/reboot. It’s tongue and cheek about its own existence, but much like the Sequel Trilogy of Star Wars, there was a fitting conclusion and it wasn’t necessary to reopen the box.

title: The Maze Runner
summary: A decent enough adaptation, albeit one that had a lot of power behind its fan base. As a narrative, it poses really interesting questions, and the allusions to, and influence of Lord of the Flies is readily apparent. A new take on the story for a new age, but with some interesting twists.

title: The Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
summary: A solid enough follow up to a decent enough adaptation. This one leaves off on a cliffhanger fitting of the genre, and the lead up to it certainly doesn’t slow down. Some moments and beats are cliched as is typical of the genre but it’s not terrible by any means.

title: Midnight Mass
summary: One of Mike Flanagan’s works, Midnight Mass could arguably be one of his best, though for the depth of character exploration that catapults the story into a bizarre plunge towards terror rather than the jumpscares and unsettling themes that are a hallmark of his other works.

title: The Midnight Sky
summary: A decent enough film, its main issue is that it suffers from a predictable conclusion that might otherwise have been well worth the watch. There’s good chemistry amongst the cast at the very least, and the design work on the production is solid. The plot just needs some improvement.

title: Midway
summary: Crap. Don’t watch.

title: Mindhunter
summary: A slow, deliberate dive into the psychology of violence. It’s less about action and more about understanding, which makes it all the more unsettling. Quietly one of the more gripping series out there.

title: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning
summary: A riveting thriller; something so heart thumping and stimulating that the time flies when watching. The stunts, many being so highly publicized before the release, pay off in full with the knowledge that they’re real, that they’re the genuine thing. The actors pull their weight and throw down hard, leaving satisfaction to be the key word when it's all said and done.

title: Molly’s Game
summary: A solid flick with a great vibe and a witty attitude that owns its identity. This is a film with roles perfectly written for the actors, and it stands out as a memorable watch.

title: Godzilla
summary: It’s a modern take on a classic that probably didn’t need to be made, but as far as blockbusters go - it’s entertaining. The big twist with this take on Godzilla is the hyper focus on American everything, and trying to build a tremendously oversized narrative for what ought to be a fairly simple monster movie.

title: Kong: Skull Island
summary: With only the second installment in the franchise - the concept is already growing stale. Solid writing takes a backseat in favor of the next wild and bizarre scenario that can be dreamed up, and all of it is terribly inconsistent and unnecessary.

title: Godzilla: King of the Monsters
summary: The trend continues with things in this franchise just getting worse and worse; the desperate push for blockbuster status on bizarre concepts that just don’t make sense, and are held together with duct tape and gum, is exhausting.

title: Godzilla vs. Kong
summary: A huge shock to no one - things continue to get worse for this franchise. A cool concept made low and shallow by the need for needlessly overpowering cgi fights that seem anything but grounded.

title: Moonfall
summary: This really didn’t need to be made at all. It’s mindless and high gloss, but it’s there, and at least you won’t be bored by the fast pace.

title: Mrs. Doubtfire
summary: Simply the greatest. Robin Williams is at his finest, and plays masterfully to his greatest talents. Mrs. Doubtfire is a joy and a thrill to watch again and again and again.

title: The Mummy
summary: While not a classic in the way Casablanca might be, The Mummy is a tremendous piece of the 90s that can’t be forgotten or set aside when considering some of the greatest films. It’s campy and hysterical and tense in all the right ways, delivering a masterful blend that is worthy of the film’s enduring reputation.

title: Mickey 17
summary: Wacky, but a total thrill, it's got both the heart and the weirdness to keep you glued and chomping at the bit to find out what the hell could possibly happen next.

title: My Cousin Vinny
summary: A genuine classic and a thrill to watch, My Cousin Vinny has something of a cult following, and rightly so–it’s a bit crass and bizarre, but wholly entertaining. This is a bright notch on Joe Pesci’s belt, and it deserves to be watched over and over again.

title: Napoleon
summary: While grand in scope and breadth, the film's ambition is also its weakness. The story would have benefited from a smaller scope, a tighter focus on one of the major aspects it tried to tie together. The battles are soaring, the drama is intense, and the history is deep, but all three are left lacking given that none received their deserving focus.

title: The Nice Guys
summary: Quite possibly one of the best action comedies of its decade, The Nice Guys punches in all the right ways and leaves its audience well fulfilled, having enjoyed a thrilling mystery, well delivered humor, and lovable characters that make you want more.

title: No Hard Feelings
summary: Funny enough to get a passing grade, it’s a weird sort of movie that will raise plenty of eyebrows, but also provide a decent enough measure of good laughter. Not outstanding, but a passable choice for a comedy night.

title: Nosferatu
summary: Exactly what it was meant to be, this take on Nosferatu is a stunning epic that is sure to haunt and stalk you into the days that follow. A great choice for any night that needs a bit of horror.

title: Nosferatu: A Symphony of Horror
summary: While certainly quite something for its time, as a silent film viewed in the modern day, it doesn’t do much to draw thrills from me—that isn’t any critique of the film itself, to be clear, but rather personal taste. Objectively, it is an iconic and historic film that served as the catalyst unlike any other for a genre of film that still reigns today.

title: Nuremberg
summary: A sobering look at justice in the aftermath of unimaginable atrocity. It’s heavy, deliberate, and not always easy to watch, but it’s important. The weight of history is felt throughout.

title: Ocean’s Eleven
summary: A classic full of thrills, Ocean’s Eleven starts off a tremendous franchise of punchy, comedic heists that throw curveballs where they’re needed, and also least expected. The cast masterfully create an immersive and compelling world in which things spiral, but for every misstep there’s still a plan within a plan to be unraveled right before your eyes.

title: Ocean’s Twelve
summary: A strong follow up to Ocean’s Eleven, this one has fun twists and some great chemistry, but focuses much less on the ensemble component in favor of really fleshing out one or two narratives.

title: The Old Guard
summary: A tremendously exciting concept, with fantastic actors, wild writing, and deep, meaningful connections that really sell the concept of immortal warriors. There’s little to criticize about this film, but there’s plenty of room to hope for more in the form of sequels and spin-offs; this is a story that can go in endless directions.

title: The Old Guard 2
summary: Just pretty damn awful, through and through. They had such a high bar to clear, to be fair, but the creators of this just bit off more than they could chew.

title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
summary: There’s so much to love about this movie—everything from its quick and witty dialogue to the absurdity of the premise and the explosive finish. Tarantino once again strikes gold with a love letter to film, and all the people that make it breathe.

title: Oppenheimer
summary: A drawback to the modern day film experience is the insane amount of attention and money poured into marketing and advertising, something which, in the case of Oppenheimer, does little to the benefit of the film as a result of it being overhyped and under-delivering. While Nolan’s latest film is certainly spectacular and grand in its scope and ambition, it struggles to pull things together in a way that doesn’t drag on and on. There are many different threads being pulled upon, and each one is disadvantaged by needing to make room for the others.

title: The Others
summary: A slow-burning ghost story built entirely on atmosphere and restraint. It’s patient, deliberate, and ultimately rewarding. Less about scares, more about dread.

title: Overlord
summary: A wild blend of genres that somehow manages to hold together. It leans into its chaos with confidence, delivering both tension and outright horror. Not subtle, but very effective.

title: The Pacific
summary: Companion to the outstanding Band of Brothers series—which focused on the European theater of WWII—The Pacific is perhaps all the more heart wrenching for its boldness to explore not just the human side of conflict, but the inhuman depths of terror that war brings out. Visceral imagery, compelling depictions, and an all around outstanding production bring this much needed view into the conflict to life.

title: Pacific Rim
summary: Pacific Rim is a weird sort of staple; it’s one of those high-gloss and fast paced stories that isn’t all too deep, and more just about the epic battles. But something about Pacific Rim has staying power, and hits a soft spot that can’t easily be explained. It’s well worth the watch.

title: Panic Room
summary: There’s a curious energy to this film that is hard to anticipate; there’s both thrill and suspense, and also depth of heart that resonates. It’s a unique concept, to enclose characters in such a confined space and to design ways to keep them in, or out. This is a standout film worthy of rewatching.

title: Paranormal Activity
summary: This film was great for its time, and for the reason alone that it felt new and bold. There’s little about it rewatch that makes it stand out aside from nostalgia.

title: Pearl Harbor
summary: Pearl Harbor certainly does its own thing, regardless of the accuracy, but it stands out as a film for the dramatic punch it throws and the draw of famous faces. There are some great scenes, some ridiculous ones, but overall it’s a film that knows what it is and doesn’t try to be something else.

title: The Perfect Storm
summary: A somber, grounded look at nature’s indifference. It builds steadily rather than explosively. There’s a quiet inevitability to it.

title: Predator
summary: A joy to jump back to; it’s campy and dramatic and ridiculous and it doesn’t take itself too seriously. For a pulpy sci-fi action flick, this is a good candidate for any watch list.

title: Predator 2
summary: Oh man. Choices were made here; very interesting choices. Take that how you will. Predator 2 goes bombastic and absurd where the original film went with experimental thrills. By Predator 2, you know the alien, you know what it’s capable of, and that leaves the audience to focus on the how of the plot’s resolution, rather than waiting to be surprised by what the alien itself is.

title: Predators
summary: A wickedly fun time. A great twist on the franchise, and a really awesome way to revive it after the choices which turned Predator 2 and the AVP films into a bit of a drag.

title: The Predator
summary: Highly problematic in some areas, but otherwise a rip roaring good time; it’s got the right punch for humor and violence, with some interesting twists, great effects and action, and a generally very likeable cast which is well picked for the roles they were given. Solid film all around.

title: Prey
summary: An absolute thrill through and through. Prey sticks with what works so well about this franchise, but takes it to whole new levels that you can’t help but get pumped for. This is a clever and deeply satisfying watch.

title: Project Hail Mary
summary: A genuinely thrilling feel good film that has both some creative twists, and also a deep heart to it. Ryan Gosling is outstanding, especially when one takes into account that it’s just him for much of the film. Definitely worth a watch.

title: Pulp Fiction
summary: A defining film that reshaped how stories could be told. Its structure, dialogue, and style are all unmistakable, even decades later. It’s chaotic, but deliberately so.

title: The Purge
summary: For the start of a franchise, it does a fairly strong job of setting up the expectations: chaos, terror, and hubris being the folly of man. Those who think they’re safe behind their walls rarely are, and when reality comes crashing in, it sets up the stakes quite clearly. Some beats of this film are shaky at best, but overall it does a great job of establishing itself in a crowded market.

title: The Purge: Anarchy
summary: Predictable violence, shallow plot, but exactly what you want if you’re watching a sequel in this franchise.

title: The First Purge
summary: A fairly safe choice of a film that, while somewhat hamfisted in its messaging about the persecution and societal disdain towards lower class minority communities, still provides for a decent enough night of entertainment. As one should expect with the Purge series, there’s lots of violence, action, and thrills.

title: Purple Hearts
summary: Pretty average. Acting isn’t great, plot is pretty jumbled, but if it’s on in the background a few scenes might be worth the time.

title: A Quiet Place
summary: A unique and exciting take on horror and thrill; it asks a question that few movies in this genre will—what if the characters actually tried to do things right? Seeing characters struggle to live by a golden rule when everything around them tempts them to break it makes for an incredibly engaging experience.

title: A Quiet Place Part II
summary: A very strong follow up to A Quiet Place, it takes things a step further and delivers on giving the viewer more of what made the first movie so good. It finds new ways to expand the concept without feeling stale.

title: A Quiet Place: Day One
summary: A stunningly well paced and executed vision for the story of what came first. You’d expect nonstop violence, but Day One has genuine heart and thrills in equal measure, making it a strong entry in the series.

title: Rat Race
summary: This is the single greatest movie of all time, deserving of sixteen Oscars and also a Tony. You can’t go wrong with Rat Race; it’s perfect for every occasion.

title: Ready Player One
summary: A rock solid thrill that leans into familiar tropes but still delivers a fast-paced and exciting adventure. It’s enjoyable both as an adaptation and as a standalone experience.

title: Rebel Moon: Part One - A Child of Fire
summary: If Star Wars: A New Hope revolutionized cinema, Rebel Moon is the exact opposite. Uninspired, poorly written, poorly acted, and terrible beyond just lackluster.

title: Renfield
summary: Absolutely hysterical. A solid banger through and through. Nicholas Hoult has carved out a niche in the vampire genre with this wild and entertaining performance.

title: The Remarkable Life of Ibelin
summary: A film that certainly strikes a chord on a personal note given my own close relationship with the content, Ibelin made a tremendous impact on a community at large, and his story is one that will resonate with many, and help to illuminate for others the depth of the world where millions go to escape.

title: Requiem for a Dream
summary: A film that leaves the viewer shell shocked. It’s less about lessons and more about how quickly things can spiral into misery. Graphic and overpowering, it leaves a lasting impression.

title: Robocop
summary: A brutal, satirical look at corporatization and identity wrapped in a sci-fi action shell. It’s far sharper than it first appears, with violence that serves a purpose.

title: Rocket Man
summary: Bombastic, wild, and matching the spirit of Elton John himself. It blends hyperbole and emotion to deliver a compelling and entertaining portrait.

title: Role Models
summary: Absolutely hysterical and deserving of praise. The cast delivers with perfect chemistry and comedic timing that makes this endlessly rewatchable.

title: Rush Hour
summary: A staple of the late 90s, Rush Hour delivers comedy and action that still holds up decades later.

title: Salt
summary: Fast, smart, and well executed. Despite a bombastic ending, the action and performances make this a standout thriller.

title: Saving Private Ryan
summary: A legendary war film that set the standard for the genre. Its realism and emotional weight make it one of the most impactful depictions of WWII ever made.

title: Saw
summary: A nostalgic entry into the horror genre that relies on brutality and twists. It works well if you don’t overthink it.

title: School of Rock
summary: Jack Black at his best. Once you buy into the premise, it becomes one of the most fun and rewatchable films out there.

title: Se7en
summary: A titan of its genre with gripping tension and a payoff that leaves the audience reeling. Exactly what a thriller should deliver.

title: Schindler’s List
summary: A movie that none should want to rate so highly, but one that deserves the praise all the same. As with all films addressing the atrocities inflicted upon the innocent during WWII, Schindler’s List dives deep into a world of misery, inhumanity, and suffering - and often leaves the viewer struggling to absorb what is depicted; all the same, it pushes on, and does quite well its job to lead the audience through the selfless endeavors of one man who risked everything to save those he could.

title: Shutter Island
summary: A solid mystery with strong twists and buildup. While it drags at times, the payoff is worth the investment.

title: Sleepy Hollow
summary: A Halloween classic that balances camp and horror. While parts haven’t aged perfectly, it still delivers an enjoyable experience.

title: The Social Dilemma
summary: A powerful look at the dangers of social media and modern technology. While some dramatizations feel heavy-handed, the core message is important and well delivered.

title: Spiderhead
summary: While this film does well with a small cast in a small set area that gets heavy usage, it’s still far short of being a hit. That said, it’s not a complete waste of time. The story is drawn from a rather interesting concept, and the avenue of human experimentation is certainly one that deserves exploration, but Spiderhead goes about revealing its twists in a way that doesn’t exactly do the concept justice.

title: Starship Troopers
summary: A prescient and wildly entertaining sci-fi film that blends action with satire. It’s both explosive and cleverly ironic.

summary: The single greatest triumph within the Star Wars franchise. A sharp, politically charged story that feels incredibly relevant and powerful.

title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
summary: A standout entry in the Star Wars franchise, proving the universe can go deeper and darker while still delivering an incredible story.

title: Sunshine
summary: A unique thriller where the tension comes from atmosphere and inevitability rather than a traditional villain. Deeply engaging and thought-provoking.

title: Super 8
summary: A heartfelt throwback to classic adventure films, blending nostalgia with modern storytelling in a satisfying way.

title: Talk to Me
summary: A fresh take on possession horror that feels grounded in modern culture. It escalates quickly but maintains emotional weight.

title: Tenet
summary: A thrilling mind-bending experience that demands attention but rewards it with complexity and spectacle.

title: The Terminator
summary: A classic that still holds up remarkably well. It set the standard for the franchise and remains a compelling watch.

title: Terminator 2: Judgement Day
summary: A fantastic sequel that builds on the original with heart, action, and a compelling character dynamic.

title: The Tomorrow War
summary: A fresh take on time travel and alien invasion with strong visuals and engaging action, though some character elements fall short.

title: Top Gun
summary: A product of its time with strong aesthetics but a shallow plot that doesn’t quite land.

title: Top Gun: Maverick
summary: A phenomenal sequel that delivers on every level, improving on the original in nearly every way.

title: The Town
summary: A grounded crime thriller with strong performances and a tight narrative that keeps things engaging.

title: Train to Busan
summary: A thrilling and emotional zombie film that focuses on human relationships as much as survival.

title: Tropic Thunder
summary: Absurd, outlandish, and hilarious. A stacked cast delivers unforgettable performances in a wildly entertaining film.

title: Twister
summary: A classic disaster film with energy, charm, and memorable action sequences that still hold up.

title: Twisters
summary: A strong follow-up that builds on the original with modern effects and a compelling cast.

title: Uncharted
summary: Having never played the games and having no reference point for any of it, the film seems to be able to stand on its own, and it certainly delivers on some strong action sequences and a likable cast, though it is weighed down by rather outlandish story elements and demands a willing imagination.

title: Us
summary: A layered, unsettling film that leans just as heavily on symbolism as it does on tension. It doesn’t always land cleanly, but there’s an ambition here that makes it more than just a standard horror entry. The discomfort it creates lingers longer than the scares themselves.

title: Valkyrie
summary: A tense retelling of a well-known historical plot that still manages to feel urgent despite the inevitable outcome. It’s methodical in its pacing, focusing more on the precision of the plan than spectacle. There’s a quiet respect in how it handles the material.

title: Van Helsing
summary: An over-the-top monster mash that fully commits to its spectacle, for better and worse. It’s chaotic, stylized, and not particularly concerned with restraint, but there’s a certain charm in that excess. Best enjoyed when you let it be exactly what it is.

title: Vantage Point
summary: An interesting concept built around perspective, though the repetition can wear thin as it unfolds. There are moments where it clicks and builds genuine tension, but it doesn’t always sustain it. Still, it’s a solid enough watch with a few clever ideas.

title: V for Vendetta
summary: A stylized, politically charged story that leans heavily into its themes of control, resistance, and identity. It’s at times heavy-handed, but also undeniably compelling in what it’s trying to say. The ideas carry as much weight as the action itself.

title: Venom
summary: A strange, uneven entry that never quite decides what it wants to be, yet somehow finds its footing through sheer personality. The dynamic between Eddie and Venom carries the film more than the plot itself ever does. It’s messy, but there’s an odd charm to it that makes it more enjoyable than it probably should be.

title: Volcano
summary: A thrilling adventure into the depths of a cataclysm erupting in the heart of L.A., Volcano has all the bang and flair with the grounded and masterful acting to back it up. This is a classic that holds up into the 2020s.

title: Walk Hard: The Dewey Cox Story
summary: A ridiculous, sharp parody that somehow manages to be both absurd and oddly insightful about the genre it’s mocking. It commits fully to the bit, and that commitment pays off again and again. There’s a surprising level of cleverness beneath all the chaos.

title: Wanted
summary: A stylized action film that leans heavily into its own concept, sometimes to the point of excess. There are moments where it feels inventive and engaging, but others where it pushes just a bit too far to fully land. Entertaining, though not entirely consistent.

title: War of the Worlds
summary: A bombastic, non-stop sort of take on the historic classic. It’s got good heart coming from its cast, and while much is glossed over, it suits the pacing, and delivers a thrilling ride.

title: Warfare
summary: A fairly standup and standout action fill, it throws viewers into the unwinnable situation where even winning comes at a cost, and the heroes end up leaving with more baggage than they went in with. Gripping and grounded.

title: War Horse
summary: Often times dark and suffocating, but other times uplifting and soaring, War Horse carefully threads a series of needles that will leave any audience unsure of whether the characters will make it through, a thrill much welcomed, but one that can perhaps push some audiences too far over the edge.

title: Watchmen
summary: A dense, visually striking adaptation that doesn’t shy away from the darker edges of its source material. It’s heavy, deliberate, and at times almost too faithful, but there’s a clear respect for the world it’s bringing to life. Not an easy watch, but a worthwhile one.

title: We Were Soldiers
summary: A grounded, emotionally charged portrayal of war that focuses as much on those at home as those on the battlefield. It carries a sense of respect and weight throughout, never losing sight of the human cost. A difficult but meaningful watch.

title: Wet Hot American Summer
summary: A classic for comedy, one that harps on the very real, and often exceptionally stupid elements of summer camp that only someone who’s attended will be able to call out. It hems close to reality, while exaggerating elements to soaring heights that will leave you grinning from ear to ear.

title: When Harry Met Sally…
summary: A film deserving of attention from open to close. There’s a despicable chemistry between the characters which is so expertly drawn out through the tremendous efforts of the actors. It’s a very unconventional love story, but one that feels more genuine and honest because of how awful the relationship is at times.

title: Who Framed Roger Rabbit
summary: A wildly inventive blend of animation and live-action that still feels impressive decades later. It’s playful on the surface, but there’s a surprisingly sharp edge beneath it. Creative, memorable, and genuinely unique.

title: Wicked
summary: An absolute triumph of an adaptation that takes the musical to new heights. If misogyny, bigotry, and the oppression of minority demographics is gravity, then Wicked certainly is defying it by boldly putting its stamp on the issues with a thunderous roar. The cast are perfectly chosen and truly do embody the energy and personality of the originating cast from the show.

title: The Wolf of Wall Street
summary: Bizarre, explosive, hysterical, and hyperbolic beyond any sense or reason; this film takes things far beyond where anyone might expect. There are no limits to the level of insanity you’ll find while watching, and that chaotic energy is something which will keep the audience guessing, and wholly ensnared by the production. This film is genuine gold.

title: World War Z
summary: A great take on the convergence of plague and disaster, and the zombie apocalypse. It’s a heart pumping, non-stop adventure that takes you all across the globe, and poses an interesting presentation of the world at the end of days.

title: Zero Day
summary: Just average. Zero Day builds on the fears of technology and political overreach, both of which are topical and legitimate. It does, however, spoil that thrill with a less than rewarding conclusion that doesn’t really line up with the demand it makes of its viewers.

title: Zodiac
summary: One of the great thriller mysteries, it’s got a fantastic, outstanding cast that knows just how to twist the knife in your gut and leave you gasping for breath.

title: 28 Days Later
summary: A tremendous classic that still holds its weight, this is a film that—like many zombie flicks before it—aims to both awe and shock with its scope and scale, but also tell a meaningful story about the survival and very human encounters of those living in the apocalypse. 28 Days Later makes the human villains just as scary, if not more so, than the actual zombies themselves, and it builds to a tremendous thrill.

title: 28 Weeks Later
summary: A strong follow up to the classic, 28 Days Later, it’s a much more modern take on the story that leans into more bombastic combat scenes with a heavily militaristic angle, compared to the grounded, civilian-centered approach to the original.

1917 is a very new, teeth cutting sort of take on the genre of war films. Set during WWI, as opposed to the much more common WWII, the film tries to imagine a condensed world where the viewers, and the characters, are subjected to the horrors of war within a limited window of time, with high stakes, devastating losses, and heart pumping thrills that are delivered with stunning performances and productions.

title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
summary: A timeless classic, this slow grinding adventure through space is one of a kind, and broke ground which built for it the reputation that endures even today. Still well worth a watch, especially for fans of film.

title: 21 Bridges
summary: A fairly strong contender when it comes to action packed thrills; there’s some good tension that builds, curious twists, and lots of great action, but the ending feels somewhat rushed.

title: Shelter
summary: It’s Jason Statham, and everything you should have come to expect from it. Wild and kick ass, the plot follows the action, but it doesn’t run exceptionally deep.

title: The Social Network
summary: Though heavily dramatized, The Social Network is an engaging and thrilling watch that charts a path through the intricacies and complexities of creation, ownership, and competing visions for the future of technology.

title: Speed
summary: One of the greats, and counted among one of my favorite films. There’s tons of exciting moments and gripping tension which stick their landing, no matter how absurd they tend to be.

title: Spies Like Us
summary: While many would pit Austin Powers as the go to spy thriller parody, Spies Like Us should be the real household name for the fact alone that it drills hard on all the right comedy elements without being completely absurdist. The actors play the part well, the chemistry between them will leave the audience in stitches.

title: Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
summary: A triumph both of its time, and of cinema history across the ages, Peter Jackson’s take on the Lord of the Rings soars, with an epic score, stunning visuals, grounded practical effects, and state of the art CGI that is subtle enough to stand up even two decades later. LoTR is a complex narrative with many perspectives and lots of things left unclear; knitting them together into a coherent story is no small feat, and Jackson has mastered it.

title: Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers
summary: The success of the first film carries on in the second, pulling on the many threads carefully placed in the Fellowship. The moments feel deeper, the relationships more gripping, and the stakes never higher. This is a fitting sequel, and a tremendous achievement even on its own.

title: Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
summary: If perfection in a second sequel could be achieved, the Return of the King is the shining example of ‘most deserving’ of the claim. The final chapters of the story will leave you with a broad mix of emotions that both collide and fuse together; it’s not an easy thing to say farewell, both for the characters and the viewers, and that struggle is felt. Every waking moment of it.

title: Abduction
summary: Generic and typical in most ways; this is a film that’s tried to leap into an already packed market while building off the hype of a young star. There’s nothing truly special about the film, and it feels hollow and stale more than it doesn’t.

title: The Angry Birds Movie 2
summary: Was forced to watch under duress while babysitting. Painful.

title: Blade
summary: The OG as far as Marvel films are concerned. Blade kicked off a revival that has carried us all into an age of thrills, providing not just the finances but the reassurance that such comic book films are greatly desired. Blade is iconic in its style, punchy action, explosive deaths, and over the top concepts, all knit together with Wesley Snipes’ signature dry humor and nonplussed aura.

title: Blade II
summary: A strong follow up to the 98’ original, Blade II is a thrill to watch for all the same reasons as the first one was. It tries to up the stakes and play on heart strings, some of which doesn’t resonate as well as it could, but it is by no means a weak film.
